name: Quantifind
summary: Quantifind is an AI-native risk intelligence company that helps financial institutions and government agencies detect, assess and mitigate financial crime and national security threats. The company's Graphyte™ platform combines internal and external data with purpose-built language models that power an agentic middleware for entity resolution and risk discovery.

name: Runpod
summary: Runpod provides a flexible, self-serve software platform for developers to access AI compute infrastructure with low minimum commitments. The company's AI Developer Cloud platform supports the full lifecycle of AI model adoption, helping developers organize, optimize and orchestrate training and inference.

name: Stay22
summary: Stay22 is a content monetization company that helps creators, publishers and brands convert audience intent into revenue. Powered by AI-driven decisioning, the Stay22 platform analyzes real-time contextual and behavioral signals to route users to relevant online travel agencies and retailers, helping drive value for creators, audiences and suppliers.

name: Veriforce
summary: Veriforce is a global leader in contractor management and supply chain risk management. The company's integrated SaaS platform combines technology, data and industry expertise to help organizations manage third-party contractors and suppliers. Through a trusted global network connecting hiring organizations, contractors and safety professionals, Veriforce streamlines compliance oversight, reduces risk and drives safer, more efficient operations. In 2025, Veriforce acquired Summit-backed Highwire, a leading provider of prequalification and contractor risk solutions for the U.S. construction and capital projects market.

name: Dreampath Diagnostics
summary: Dreampath Diagnostics is a global leader in digital sample management systems for anatomical pathology laboratories. The company's patented solutions combine purpose-built scanners, software and consumables to automate the archiving and retrieval of tissue samples, improving lab efficiency, accuracy and compliance.

name: Quorso
summary: Quorso offers an intelligent management platform that helps multi-store retailers improve performance by transforming complex operational data into personalized, high-impact actions for frontline managers and store teams. By integrating sales, labor and inventory insights into a single intelligent workflow, Quorso empowers retailers to align store operations, improve productivity and deliver tangible results.

name: RIS Rx
summary: RIS Rx is a healthcare technology company delivering real-time gross-to-net (GTN) revenue protection for some of the world's leading pharmaceutical manufacturers. Through proprietary software, real-time analytics and pharmacist-led expertise, RIS Rx helps clients optimize operations, eliminate leakage and ensure that financial assistance reaches the patients who need it most.

name: Instrumentl
summary: Instrumentl is a grant discovery and management platform for nonprofit organizations. Its integrated technology is designed to intelligently match grant seekers with providers, streamlining grant discovery, application tracking and reporting to help nonprofits secure funding more efficiently.

name: Manychat
summary: Manychat is a marketing automation and customer conversation platform designed to enable businesses and creators to automate personalized outreach at scale across social media platforms. Manychat helps users convert online audiences into paying customers, offering easy-to-use, differentiated solutions focused on building consumer audiences, lowering digital acquisition costs and generating sales.

name: Simply.TV
summary: Simply.TV is a global leader in metadata and content discovery solutions, providing state-of-the-art services to some of the world's most prominent TV entertainment brands. The company's platform aggregates and enriches metadata, combining AI, machine learning and editorial workflows to deliver accurate, up-to-date metadata, enabling TV operators, broadcasters and streaming services to offer personalized and intuitive user experiences.

name: One Point Advisory Services
summary: One Point Advisory Services is a platform that supports regional accounting and independent financial adviser firms across the United Kingdom. It offers a long-term, stable ownership model, enabling partner firms to maintain their independence while providing a scalable platform for growth, succession planning and partner incentivization.

name: Cenosco
summary: Cenosco is a leading provider of asset integrity management (AIM) software designed to enhance safety, reliability and operational efficiency in asset-heavy industries. The company's Integrity Management Suite (IMS) offers mission-critical integrity, reliability and process safety solutions to some of the world's largest oil & gas, chemical and power companies.

name: Fundraise Up
summary: Fundraise Up is a premier fundraising platform for nonprofits designed to address the inefficiencies and complexities of traditional online donation processes. Using predictive AI to drive donor experiences, Fundraise Up helps nonprofits of any size engage more supporters and grow revenue, offering digital tools to increase conversion, add modern payment methods and personalize the giving experience.

name: UHY
summary: UHY is one of the largest accounting, tax, consulting and advisory services firms in the United States, serving a diverse client base ranging from closely held middle-market companies to Fortune 500 firms. UHY serves clients across numerous high-growth industries, providing tailored solutions designed to meet each business's unique needs.

name: HyperLight
summary: HyperLight is a leading provider of thin film lithium niobate (TFLN) photonic integrated circuits (PICs). HyperLight's TFLN PICs deliver unmatched bandwidth and energy efficiency well-aligned with the current and future needs of AI/data center infrastructure, telecommunications optical networks and high-performance computing.

name: Earned Wealth
summary: Earned Wealth is the first comprehensive, tech-enabled financial services firm purpose-built for the complex needs of healthcare professionals and medical practices. Offering wealth, tax, career advisory, insurance, and other services, Earned uses a doctor's unique career journey — from residency to buying into private practice to retirement — as a lens for the company's data-driven, evidence-based recommendations.

name: Revizto
summary: Revizto is a leading provider of collaboration solutions for the Architecture, Engineering, Construction and Operation (AECO) industry. The company's user-friendly, cloud-based platform is purpose-built to enable architects, engineers, contractors and owners to work and communicate in a unified 3D and 2D environment. The platform – accessible in-office, on-site or on any device – is utilized globally for real-time coordination, automated clash detection and issue tracking throughout the lifecycle of a building or infrastructure project.

name: Celcoin
summary: Celcoin is a Banking as a Service (“BaaS”) technology provider, offering financial infrastructure solutions – including payments, digital banking and lending applications – for banks, fintechs and enterprise customers. Celcoin makes it easy for its customers to deliver personalized embedded finance solutions for consumers at any stage of development.

name: PostPilot
summary: PostPilot is a tech-enabled direct-mail marketing company serving ecommerce and omnichannel brands. PostPilot offers an innovative, data-driven direct mail platform that helps brands acquire and retain customers with smart, AI-driven, personalized postcard and catalog campaigns. With native integrations to the ecommerce solutions that DTC brands use most, PostPilot allows brands of all sizes to leverage their owned customer data to market at scale, unimpeded by ad networks and other intermediaries.

name: OneStock SAS
summary: OneStock is a leading provider of AI-driven distributed order management solutions that help brands, retailers and B2B merchants deliver seamless omnichannel fulfillment at scale. The company's cloud-native platform provides real-time, unified inventory visibility and intelligent order orchestration, helping brands increase sales, improve operational efficiency and enhance customer experiences.

name: Netgain
summary: Netgain provides technical accounting and accounting automation software designed to simplify, standardize and automate complex accounting processes. Netgain's products extend the capabilities of NetSuite, Quickbooks and audit firms, allowing customers to have more fully featured and integrated accounting capabilities for their business.

name: Fortify Companies
summary: Fortify Companies (“Fortify”) is a national property services network focused on improving how building assets are protected and restored. Through the Insurcomm and Rytech Restoration networks, Fortify provides coordinated services, including proactive loss prevention, rapid disaster response, property damage restoration and remediation, and facility management solutions, designed to support property owners and insurers across a wide range of environments.

name: CollisionRight
summary: CollisionRight is a leading provider of automotive collision repair services through a network of high-quality, customer-friendly facilities across the Central U.S. and Mid-Atlantic regions. The company differentiates itself through a passion for quality repair work, backed by heavy investment in state-of-the-art equipment, facilities, technician training and auto manufacturer certifications, while maintaining strong relationships with insurance carriers.

name: DocuSketch
summary: DocuSketch is a leading provider of process optimization solutions purpose-built for the property restoration industry. The company's all-in-one, easy-to-use solution features 360° documentation, automated scoping and sketching solutions, and estimating services designed to automate and streamline critical claims processes.

name: CERTANIA
summary: CERTANIA is a provider of Testing, Inspection, and Certification (TIC) services focused on addressing global challenges across health and sustainability. Formed by uniting leading partner companies in TIC industry, CERTANIA offers an extensive portfolio of services including food, environmental and industrial testing, CRO services, ESG analytics and consulting, and a broad range of certification and quality-control services.

name: Furnished Finder
summary: Furnished Finder is a leading specialty online real estate listing platform for furnished rentals with 30+ day minimum stays. The company offers a large and unique inventory of furnished, multi-month rentals by owner, catering primarily to traveling professionals on work assignments and families who are relocating or renovating their homes. Furnished Finder does not charge booking fees or commissions, allowing landlords to retain full control over their rentals, ultimately making the process more affordable for tenants and more profitable for property owners.

name: Hallmark Health Care Solutions
summary: Hallmark Health Care Solutions offers a SaaS-based workforce management platform designed to help health systems, hospitals, physician groups and other healthcare organizations address labor-related challenges. The company's platform combines tools focused on talent sourcing and deployment with provider compensation and contracting to create an end-to-end solution that helps drive transparency, efficiency and cost savings.

name: TradingHub
summary: In an environment of evolving regulatory expectations and increasingly complex trading strategies, TradingHub was built to address the growing limitations of traditional, rules-based surveillance tools. The company's platform uses advanced statistical and financial models across asset classes to analyze trading behavior at scale and produces risk-related signals. TradingHub had grown to serve a global customer base, including Tier-1 banks, asset managers and hedge funds, supported by a deeply technical, engineering-led culture.

name: Keeper Security
summary: Keeper is a leading provider of zero-trust and zero-knowledge cybersecurity software used by thousands of organizations and millions of individuals worldwide. The company offers a next-generation cybersecurity platform for password management, secrets management, privileged access, secure remote access and encrypted messaging.

name: Doctrine
summary: Doctrine is a leading provider of data, search and productivity solutions for legal professionals. The Doctrine platform aggregates, contextualizes and automates the processing of legal information and is designed to empower legal professionals to conduct more accurate and efficient search and save time, limit errors and focus on higher value-add tasks.

name: LumaBridge
summary: LumaBridge is a specialty clinical research organization (CRO) focused on creating innovative clinical trial solutions for biopharma companies. Founded in 2014 as Cancer Insight, the company designs and executes outsourced clinical trials. LumaBridge offers strategic partnership and personalized attention to each sponsor, accelerating the development of potential therapies.

name: QAD
summary: QAD is a leading provider of next-generation manufacturing and supply chain solutions in the cloud to enable the Adaptive Enterprise. The company offers a wide range of enterprise solutions including enterprise resource planning (ERP), digital commerce (DC), supplier relationship management (SRM), digital supply chain planning (DSCP), global trade and transportation execution (GTTE), and enterprise quality management system (EQMS).

name: US Health Partners
summary: U.S. Health Partners (“USHP”) is a national cardiovascular care platform that seeks to provide its network of physicians with resources to help maintain their clinical autonomy, grow their practices and improve the quality of patient care.

name: Imagen
summary: Imagen is a leading provider of AI-powered image editing solutions for professional photographers. Designed to intelligently automate image editing at scale, Imagen's solutions remove the tedious aspects of post-production work and allow each artist to maintain and evolve his or her own creative style.

name: Chronograph
summary: Chronograph is a leading provider of next-generation technology solutions for the private capital market. Through a suite of cloud-based analytics and data management solutions, Chronograph helps institutional limited partners and general partners – including many of the world's largest private equity and venture capital investors – streamline and automate portfolio monitoring, valuations, analytics, and reporting.

name: Deine Urologie
summary: Deine Urologie is a physician-led network of urology practices across Germany. The group combines leading medical expertise, modern infrastructure and interdisciplinary collaboration to deliver high-quality care for patients. With an integrated model designed to improve access, coordination and outcomes, Deine Urologie supports greater efficiency, transparency and compliance across its practices.

name: DERMANOVIS
summary: DERMANOVIS is a physician-founded network of dermatology practices in Germany. The group brings together leading medical expertise, advanced treatments and collaborative practice models to provide comprehensive care spanning medical dermatology, aesthetic procedures, laser medicine and medical cosmetics. Through its partnership approach, DERMANOVIS supports practice succession and fosters long-term collaboration, allowing physicians to focus on delivering high-quality patient care.

name: Energy Aspects
summary: Energy Aspects is a provider of energy data analysis and market intelligence to a global client base. Drawing on extensive proprietary data models, the company offers in-depth coverage of market fundamentals, price movements and geopolitical context to help guide investment, trading and strategic decisions.

name: StackAdapt
summary: StackAdapt was founded in 2014 with a vision to make programmatic advertising more accessible, intelligent and effective. Drawing on first-hand expertise in digital media and engineering, the founders worked to build a next-generation, self-serve platform that leverages data, automation and machine learning to help give marketers more control and transparency. By 2022, StackAdapt was growing profitably and rapidly, with a global customer base and an ambitious roadmap spanning connected TV, mobile and audio. To help further scale international operations and continue product innovation, the founders sought a long-term growth partner with deep experience supporting leading software businesses.

name: TextExpander
summary: TextExpander is a developer of productivity software designed to automate content creation through highly customizable, user-defined shortcuts or "snippets." From small teams to enterprise-wide deployments, users leverage TextExpander across a variety of job functions and use cases – from customer support to recruiting to outbound sales.

name: Halborn
summary: Halborn is an emerging leader in the blockchain cybersecurity space. The company offers a comprehensive suite of security solutions for companies developing applications on blockchains including DevSecOps management, smart contract audits and advanced penetration testing.

name: PriceLabs
summary: PriceLabs is a provider of revenue management SaaS solutions primarily for owners and managers of short-term rental ("STR") properties.

name: Foxen
summary: Foxen is a leading provider of innovative financial technology solutions for real estate, serving multifamily property owners, operators, managers and their residents. Foxen's integrated products include an automated compliance monitoring and renters insurance program, as well as a rent payment reporting and credit building program for tenants.

name: 48forty Solutions
summary: 48forty Solutions is a leading national provider of recycled wood pallets and related total pallet management services operating across the United States and Canada. The company provides end-to-end pallet solutions from supply to retrieval, new and custom pallets, and recycling and reverse logistics services.

name: FNZ
summary: FNZ is a global wealth management platform that partners with the world's leading financial institutions to transform the way the industry serves its clients. FNZ provides an integrated technology, transaction and custody platform to wealth managers, banks, insurers and asset managers in 18 countries across North America, Europe, Africa and Asia-Pacific.

name: Later
summary: Later is a leading influencer marketing company, focused on helping brands create high-impact campaigns with confidence. Built on a native, AI-powered platform and more than a decade of proprietary data, Later combines trusted insight with expert guidance to remove guesswork from influencer marketing, enabling brands to choose the right creators, execute fully managed campaigns and drive meaningful growth.

name: Cinerius Financial Partners
summary: Cinerius is a consolidation platform focused on growing independent wealth management firms in the German-speaking region of Europe ("DACH"). The company seeks to promote a higher growth dynamic by offering resources at scale and strategic support to acquired firms while allowing leaders to maintain operational independence.

name: Invicti Security
summary: Invicti is a global leader in web application security, offering dynamic and interactive application security testing products serving IT, security and development teams. The company's platform is designed to provide a comprehensive view of an organization's entire web application portfolio and enable enterprises to continually scan and secure all of their web applications and APIS to preemptively identify and remediate vulnerabilities before software is released into production.

name: Optimove
summary: Optimove is a leading provider of marketing automation and optimization software designed to empower marketing teams to create and manage large-scale, customer-led journeys. Optimove's CRM Journey mapping algorithm leverages AI to autonomously surface valuable customer engagement opportunities, orchestrate self-optimizing customer journeys, and accurately measure the incremental impact of all marketing interactions.

name: Hairstory
summary: Hairstory is a clean haircare brand that utilizes natural and sustainable ingredients to produce products designed to simplify the haircare regimen. The company's core product, New Wash, cleans hair without any detergent, ending what the company sees as a vicious cycle of over-cleansing created by shampoo. Hairstory uses non-toxic, biodegradable ingredients and refillable packaging, and a portion of every New Wash purchase is donated to water-related conservation nonprofits.

name: TurningPoint Healthcare Solutions
summary: TurningPoint Healthcare Solutions provides an innovative suite of specialty care management services and technologies focused on managing complex conditions including musculoskeletal (MSK), ENT, sleep, and cardiac care. The company engages constituents across the continuum of care to create transparency and alignment of patients, providers, and payors to drive high-quality, cost-effective patient care.

name: Dr. Squatch
summary: Dr. Squatch is a natural men's soap and personal care company. The brand's hero products include natural cold process bar soap, body wash, deodorant, hair care, lip balm and cologne.

name: Formative
summary: Formative provides a SaaS platform used by K-12 teachers to create digital exercises and assessments to review student performance. Teachers and administrators use Formative to evaluate students' work in real-time, make instant teaching adjustments, track long-term student growth and drive strategic curriculum decisions.

name: OTR Solutions
summary: OTR Solutions provides industry-leading technology and financial services, tools, and support to small and medium-sized trucking companies. The company offers a wide range of services for the trucking community, including financing and back-office solutions, a dedicated fuel team, news and education, and innovative technology to help carriers and brokers start and grow successful operations.

name: Veranex
summary: Veranex is a global provider of comprehensive, tech-enabled services to the medical technology industry. Offering expert guidance for each of its four concept-through-commercialization pillars — engineering and design, clinical, market access, and regulatory — Veranex is designed to enable accelerated speed to market, controlled development costs, development risk mitigation and accelerated market viability assessment.

name: Lingoda
summary: Lingoda is a leading online language learning school that provides live, small group and private classes in four distinct languages. The company's online language classes are available 24 hours a day and are taught by a community of more than 1,400 certified, native-speaking teachers.

name: Highwire
summary: Highwire (formerly known as ConstructSecure) is the Contractor Success platform for managing risk in capital project construction and operations. Enterprises use Highwire's SaaS platform to ensure their contracting partners deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality and sustainability risks throughout every project lifecycle.

name: Ylopo
summary: Ylopo is an all-in-one marketing automation platform that helps real estate agents and mortgage intermediaries build their online presence and expand their client acquisition funnel. The company's suite of automated marketing, client conversion, client management and team management tools help brokers manage their business and improve productivity.

name: Fracture
summary: Fracture is a direct-to-consumer ("DTC") designer and manufacturer of wall art printed directly on glass. The company creates affordable and sustainable products that allows anyone to print photos on simple pieces of glass and easily hang them in their living spaces.

name: MUBI
summary: MUBI was founded in 2007 with a mission to bring the world's greatest films to audiences everywhere. Frustrated by the lack of access to cinema classics and global prestige film titles, founder Efe Cakarel set out to build a more curated, intentional experience for film lovers. What began as a boutique streaming service has grown into a global media company with an integrated ecosystem spanning streaming, content production, theatrical distribution and film sales.

name: Solactive
summary: Solactive is a technology-enabled provider of indices and index solutions to the global financial services industry. Offering a high level of flexibility, speed and value, the company develops tailor-made index solutions for ETFs and other index-linked investments across equity, fixed income and multi-asset class strategies

name: RWA Wealth Partners
summary: RWA Wealth Partners provides wealth management solutions integrated seamlessly with tax, estate and family office services. The firm has a deep bench of advisors with the expertise and technology to tackle the most complex financial problems for high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families across the country.

name: ShipMonk
summary: ShipMonk is a leading provider of tech-enabled fulfillment and inventory management solutions to SMB ecommerce companies. The company's proprietary software powers its fulfillment services.

name: Syndigo
summary: Syndigo is the first Active Content Engine built to power the continual flow of data and content throughout the entire commerce ecosystem. With Syndigo, brands, retailers and distributors are able to control all content and data flows, harness data growth, continuously optimize data, and get everything they need – including PIM, MDM, syndication and analytics capabilities – to improve decision-making and accelerate sales on every shelf.

name: Jobber
summary: Jobber is an all-in-one SaaS business management platform for small home service businesses. Jobber helps small businesses provide 5-star service at scale by automating day-to-day operations, including managing quote requests, proposal generation, job dispatching, invoicing, payment collection and retention marketing.

name: VaxCare
summary: VaxCare was founded in 2007 with a vision to help physicians modernize their vaccine programs. Initially focused on small and mid-sized clinics, the company offered workflow tools designed to reduce the administrative burden of vaccine delivery and to streamline tasks like inventory management, eligibility tracking and reimbursement. Seeing an opportunity to evolve into a more integrated platform purpose-built to serve the complex needs of larger physician groups and health systems, the VaxCare team sought a growth partner to help expand its product, scale operations and reposition its go-to-market strategy for broader impact.

name: Redzone
summary: Redzone provides a connected worker platform designed to optimize productivity, safety and quality on the plant floor. The company's software and coaching help manufacturing front-line employees implement best practices, collaborate and resolve unexpected issues.

name: GRESB
summary: GRESB is a mission-driven and industry-led organization providing standardized and validated Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) data to financial markets. Established in 2009, GRESB has become the leading ESG benchmark for real estate and infrastructure investments across the world, used by institutional and financial investors to inform decision-making.

name: Fortis Life Sciences
summary: Fortis Life Sciences is a global platform that provides capital, expertise and operational resources to grow founder-led life sciences tools companies serving diagnostic, therapeutic and research customers.

name: Beyond Risk
summary: Beyond Risk is an independent alternative risk insurance services company focused on helping middle-market and SMB clients manage exposures and reduce the total cost of risk. The company offers a comprehensive portfolio of alternative risk solutions including property & casualty program management, healthcare employee benefits solutions and captive management.

name: Leon Medical Centers
summary: Leon Medical Centers is the largest independent, integrated provider network serving Medicare Advantage members in Miami-Dade County, Florida. Leon's care delivery network includes medical centers, healthy living centers, a network of exclusive specialists and a medical infrastructure of urgent care, home health services, on-site pharmacies and labs, transportation services and disease management.

name: LearnUpon
summary: LearnUpon is a leading cloud-based Learning Management System (LMS) used by enterprises to deliver high-quality corporate learning. The company's integrated, easy-to-use platform is designed to seamlessly administer, deliver and report on training content consumed by multiple audiences, including internal employees and external customers and partners.

name: Output
summary: Output offers a SaaS-based digital music creation platform designed to help musicians easily manipulate and customize sounds in real time. Musicians at all levels – from professionals to first-time hobbyists – rely on Output's creative tools and proprietary library of 40,000+ loops to find inspiration and break through writer's block.

name: CluePoints
summary: CluePoints was founded in 2012 with a vision to provide pharmaceutical companies and CROs a better way of identifying, visualizing, managing and documenting risks that could impact the outcome of clinical trials. The company's cloud-based Risk-Based Quality Management ("RBQM") solutions help sponsors remotely monitor clinical and operational data in real-time to increase efficiency, improve patient safety, and reduce costs. By 2020, CluePoints was seen as an established leader in RBQM, and the Co-founders sought a partner with deep experience scaling healthcare software businesses to support the company's continued growth and global expansion to meet accelerating customer demand worldwide.

name: Appway
summary: Appway is a leading provider of business process automation software focused on customer onboarding and customer lifecycle management for financial institutions globally. Appway's software is designed to digitize, automate and accelerate core business processes to boost internal efficiencies in compliance with complex industry regulations, while creating exceptional customer experiences at every touchpoint.

name: Brooklinen
summary: Brooklinen is a direct-to-consumer designer and retailer of stylish, luxury-grade home essentials delivered to customers at accessible prices. Brooklinen offers a highly curated online shopping experience for bedding, bath goods, loungewear and home accessories, with a focus on quality and exceptional customer experience.

name: Recharge
summary: Recharge is a leading provider of subscription billing software for e-commerce. Merchants of all sizes use Recharge's billing and payment management solutions to grow their business by increasing customer lifetime value and reducing customer churn.

name: eClinical Solutions
summary: eClinical Solutions is a leading global provider of cloud-based enterprise SaaS and software-driven clinical data services. The company's elluminate® platform is designed to increase clinical trial efficiency and help accelerate the clinical development process by providing life sciences companies with greater control of their clinical trial data.

name: InnovaCare Health
summary: InnovaCare Health is one of the largest integrated, value-based payor and provider service organizations in the United States. Through an integrated portfolio of health plans, medical service organizations and clinical networks, InnovaCare works across the healthcare ecosystem – with patients, providers, payors and other partners – to increase access to high-quality, affordable care.

name: Odoo
summary: Founded in 2005 by Fabien Pinckaers, Odoo was built to give businesses of all sizes access to powerful, flexible software traditionally reserved for large enterprises. By 2019, the company had grown to serve more than 4.5 million users worldwide through an open-core model that combined Odoo's suite of proprietary enterprise applications with thousands of apps contributed by Odoo's global community-built apps.

name: CoderPad
summary: CoderPad offers a SaaS application used for conducting remote, collaborative, realistic technical interviews of software developers and data scientists. A leader in the developer assessment tools category, CoderPad serves many of the largest technology employers as well as small- and medium-sized businesses.

name: Snap Finance
summary: Snap Finance is a provider of point-of-sale, virtual rent-to-own ("vRTO") financing programs. The company's vRTO lease programs help to facilitate consumer purchases of larger ticket items such as furniture, mattresses and bedding, auto wheels and tires, jewelry, and electronics.

name: Immersive
summary: Immersive Labs is an interactive, gamified cyber skills platform that delivers challenge-based skills content based on the latest threat intelligence. The company's innovative on-demand platform enables customers to train their workforces in up-to-date, practical cyber skills and to assess cyber competencies against industry frameworks.

name: ProGlove
summary: ProGlove is a leading provider of industrial wearables designed to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of scanning processes used across the manufacturing, logistics, transportation and ecommerce/retail sectors. The company's IoT-connected scanner-glove helps workers increase productivity and optimize workflow.

name: Akeneo
summary: Akeneo is a global leader in Product Experience Management (PXM) solutions that help merchants and brands deliver a compelling customer experience across online and offline sales channels. Akeneo's open source enterprise solutions dramatically improve product data quality and accuracy while simplifying and accelerating product catalog management.

name: Global Gruppe
summary: Global Gruppe was founded in Cologne in 1999 with a focus on delivering sophisticated commercial property and casualty ("P&C") insurance to the small and medium enterprise ("SME") market, a segment historically underserved by large brokers. The company built a regional presence through organic growth and selective acquisitions, helping to establish itself as a client-focused, founder-owned platform in the fragmented German insurance brokerage market.

name: Abode Healthcare
summary: Abode is a leading provider of home health and hospice services throughout the United States. The company offers a full spectrum of palliative care and support services that are tailored to the individual needs and preferences of terminally ill patients and their families.

name: Viroclinics Biosciences
summary: Viroclinics Biosciences is a leading provider of specialized clinical and non-clinical services to the global virology therapeutics and vaccine development market. The company offers a full range of services to support and accelerate the market entry of new drugs, vaccines and antivirals from discovery to launch.

name: RadioOnkologieNetzwerk
summary: RadioOnkologieNetzwerk ("RON") is a leading provider of multi-specialist oncology treatment services, operating one of the largest radiotherapy and oncology networks in Germany. The company is committed to delivering the highest quality treatment and service levels to cancer patients throughout Germany.

name: U.S. Renal Care
summary: U.S. Renal Care works in partnership with nephrologists to develop, acquire, and operate outpatient treatment centers for persons suffering from End Stage Renal Disease ("ESRD"). The company serves approximately 25,000 patients in more than 330 dialysis facilities across more than 30 states.

name: Allego
summary: Allego is a leader in modern revenue enablement technology. Allego's all-in-one enablement suite helps companies ready more confident sales teams, curate impactful content, and engage buyers more effectively.

name: Red Canary
summary: Red Canary is a security operations ally to organizations of all sizes, providing managed detection and response solutions that integrate behavioral analytics and automated response with 24/7/365 investigation by an expert Cyber Incident Response Team. Red Canary's cloud-based, outcome-focused solutions can be deployed in minutes to identify and shut down attacks.

name: Red Points
summary: Red Points is a leading provider of an all-in-one SaaS solution for detecting and enforcing online IP infringements, including online counterfeiting, piracy and distribution fraud. The company's AI-driven software removes more than 100,000 incidents of illegal products and content from the web daily.

name: A Cloud Guru
summary: Brothers Sam and Ryan Kroonenburg founded A Cloud Guru ("ACG") in 2015 with a mission to teach the world to cloud. At the time, the available cloud training options were both expensive and unable to keep pace with rapidly changing technology, so the pair aimed to make cloud learning relevant, accessible, affordable – and even fun.

name: Klaviyo
summary: Klaviyo powers smarter digital relationships, making it easy for businesses to capture, store, analyze, and predictively use their own data to drive measurable, high-value outcomes. Klaviyo's modern and intuitive SaaS platform enables business users of any skill level to harness their first-party data from more than 300 integrations to send the right message at the right time across email, SMS, and push notifications.

name: Markforged
summary: Markforged is a leading provider of industrial 3D printers, materials and design software. Engineers, designers, and manufacturing professionals around the world rely on Markforged metal and composite printers for tooling, fixtures, functional prototyping, and high-value end-use production.

name: Patriot Growth Insurance Services
summary: Patriot is a growth-focused national insurance services firm that partners with employee benefits and property & casualty agencies across the United States. Patriot's collaborative model delivers resources and strategic support to its agencies, whose leaders continue to operate with a high degree of autonomy in their local markets.

name: Sifted
summary: Sifted is a leading logistics data science platform that helps shippers save money by optimizing business decisions. The company combines the power of logistics intelligence software and transportation data science to help shippers navigate an increasingly complex shipping environment.

name: Syncron
summary: Syncron helps manufacturers and distributors capitalize on the new service economy by increasing customer loyalty and optimizing aftermarket profitability. The company's integrated, scalable, state-of-the-art Service Lifecycle Management (SLM) cloud platform puts data at the heart of aftermarket operations to unlock service synergies, significant revenue opportunities and help companies differentiate themselves with exceptional aftermarket customer experiences.

name: OnRobot
summary: OnRobot is a leading provider of end-of-arm tooling (EOAT) used in collaborative robot systems. OnRobot offers hardware and software that help manufacturers take full advantage of the benefits of collaborative robot applications: ease of use, cost-effectiveness and safe deployment and operation alongside human workers.

name: a.k.a. Brands
summary: a.k.a. Brands is a global, direct-to-consumer platform comprised of digitally-native fashion brands offering a curated assortment of on-trend, affordably-priced fashion apparel and accessories. The company leverages its operational expertise and deep industry experience to help emerging brands grow and achieve their full potential.

name: Sound Physicians
summary: Sound Inpatient Physicians is a physician-led multispecialty medical group providing acute and post-acute care services—including hospital medicine, emergency medicine, anesthesia, critical care, accountable care, telemedicine and practice solutions. Sound partners with hospitals and health systems, offering clinical solutions designed to drive improvements in quality, patient satisfaction and financial outcomes across the full episode of care.

name: Teaching Strategies
summary: Teaching Strategies is the largest provider of comprehensive early childhood education solutions for holistic child development, offering the leading digital early learning platform, integrating curriculum, assessment and engagement tools for educators and families. The company's proprietary, research-based solutions for development and learning span from birth to Grade 3 and cover all areas of development of a child.

name: NinjaOne
summary: NinjaOne is a leading unified IT operations solution that simplifies the way IT teams work. With NinjaOne, MSPs and IT departments can automate, manage, and remediate all their endpoint management tasks within one fast, modern, intuitive platform, improving technician efficiency and user satisfaction.

name: Elatec
summary: Elatec is a leading global provider of highly-configurable RFID (radio frequency identification) technology solutions used in a variety of end-markets and industry applications, including printing, computing, manufacturing and the Internet of Things (IoT). The company designs and manufactures both contact (SmartCard) and close proximity contactless (NFC, RFID, Bluetooth) readers/writers that enable work optimization, workforce management, compliance and security.

name: Blended Health
summary: Blended Health is a behavioral healthcare company focused on expanding access to outpatient behavioral healthcare services for adolescents and adults struggling with acute mental health challenges. Operating several outpatient clinics across the U.S. under the Connections Wellness Group brand, Blended Health offers partial hospitalization programs (PHP) and intensive outpatient programs (IOP) that provide a strong continuity of care.

name: onX
summary: Founded in 2009, onX was built to empower outdoor enthusiasts with reliable digital maps and land access data delivered through activity-specific mobile applications. After nearly a decade of profitable, founder-led growth and strong traction in the hunting segment, the company saw an opportunity to broaden its product portfolio and serve a wider set of outdoor use cases.

name: Advance Medical
summary: Advance Medical is a global provider of second opinion and other telemedicine services to large employers and insurers. With offices in Barcelona, Boston, Lisbon, Shanghai, Sao Paulo, Santiago de Chile and the UK, Advance Medical employs more than 800 dedicated health professionals worldwide and offers expert medical opinions, 24/7 medical advice, triage and a range of other telehealth services.

name: Trintech
summary: Trintech, Inc. pioneered the development of Financial Corporate Performance Management (FCPM) software to optimize the Record-to-Report (R2R) process for the office of finance. Trintech's clients rely on its integrated, end-to-end solutions to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and improve governance and transparency throughout the financial close process.

name: Jungle Scout
summary: Jungle Scout is a leading all-in-one platform used by e-commerce merchants who transact on global e-commerce marketplaces. The company's solutions feature a full suite of best-in-class business management solutions and powerful market intelligence resources to help entrepreneurs and brands manage their e-commerce businesses.

name: LakePharma
summary: LakePharma is a leading biologics company specializing in antibody engineering, antibody discovery, molecular engineering, protein chemistry, bioexpression, biofunction, bioprocessing and bioanalytics. Its services and technology enable clients to accelerate product development from discovery to Phase 1 clinical trials.

name: Normec
summary: Normec is a technology-enabled provider of Testing, Inspection and Certification (“TIC”) services in the Benelux region and Germany. Based in the Netherlands, Normec is pursuing an active consolidation strategy by acquiring independent TIC businesses and integrating them onto its structured and scalable platform.

name: Harvey Performance Company
summary: Harvey Performance Company (“HPC”) provides high-performance cutting tools for the manufacturing and metalworking industries through three complementary brands: Harvey Tool, a broad, one-stop offering for specialty micro-cutting tools; Helical Solutions, a manufacturer of high-performance custom and standard end mills; and Micro 100, a manufacturer of turning and milling tools.

name: Market Logic
summary: Founded in 2006, Market Logic provides SaaS-based Marketing Information Solutions (“MIS”) to bring the right information at the right time to the world's best marketing, research and intelligence teams. Clients include global brands from FMCG/CPG, healthcare, retail, high-tech, finance and communications sectors, and more than 600 research agencies.

name: zahneins
summary: zahneins was created by bringing together several leading dental clinics and practitioners to form one of the largest private dental networks in Germany. Partnering with local dentists, zahneins provides comprehensive practice management support that enables dentists to focus on providing the highest quality care to patients.

name: Club Champion
summary: Club Champion is one of the largest premium custom golf club fitting retailers in the world, with more than 60 locations across the United States. Club Champion uses highly trained fitters and the latest in ball flight measuring technology to deliver a Tour-quality fitting experience using an unbiased approach with the goal of producing longer, more accurate shots to lower golfers’ scores.

name: Smartsheet
summary: Smartsheet is a world-leading SaaS platform for managing and automating collaborative work. Tens of thousands of companies, and millions of information workers, across more than 190 countries trust Smartsheet to help them accelerate business execution and address the volume and velocity of today’s collaborative work.

name: MacStadium
summary: MacStadium is a leading provider of on-demand cloud infrastructure-as-a-service (“IaaS”) built on top of Mac equipment used primarily by developers building application for Apple’s mobile operating system.

name: Podium
summary: Podium helps businesses drive customer interaction in the form of online reviews to increase visibility, improve business operations, and drive purchase decisions.

name: Vensure Employer Services
summary: Vensure Employer Services (formerly PrismHR) provides innovative human capital management (HCM) solutions and services for businesses that aspire to leverage their human capital for growth. The company offers complete, end-to-end solutions for payroll, human resources, benefits, risk management and workers’ compensation services.

name: Ascentis
summary: Ascentis is a market-leading provider of cloud-based, software-as-a-service (SaaS) human capital management (HCM) solutions and integrated payroll services. Ascentis offers a comprehensive, end-to-end suite of human resources, benefits, payroll, timekeeping and talent management products that increase productivity and provide meaningful insights into organizational trends.

name: LifeStance Health
summary: LifeStance Health is a behavioral healthcare company focused on providing evidence-based, medically driven treatment services for children, adolescents and adults suffering from a variety of mental health issues in an outpatient care setting, both in-person and through its digital health telemedicine offering.

name: Medical Review Institute of America
summary: MRIoA is a leading provider of tech-enabled clinical review services for healthcare organizations designed to help patients receive the right care at the right time and in the right place, thereby improving the value equation in healthcare. The company provides independent clinical and utilization reviews, evidence-based clinical opinions and appropriate utilization analytics for many of the largest health plans, pharmacy benefit managers, third party administrators, government entities and self-insured employers in the United States.

name: HealthSun
summary: Founded in 2005, HealthSun operates one of the largest and fastest-growing integrated Medicare Advantage health plan and care delivery networks focused primarily on the dual-eligible population in Florida’s Miami-Dade and Broward counties.

name: FineLine Technologies
summary: FineLine Technologies is a leading provider of data and RFID solutions to more than 220 global retailers and consumer brands and 30,000 manufacturers worldwide. FineLine’s FastTrak – a cloud-based, end-to-end client-facing technology platform – streamlines retail logistics between suppliers and retailers, providing speed and accuracy on complex orders with a fully integrated ordering, production and tracking experience.

name: Thrive Skilled Pediatric Care
summary: Thrive Skilled Pediatric Care (Thrive SPC) is one of the largest independent providers of pediatric home care in the United States. Offering skilled private duty nursing services, pediatric therapy, licensed health aide services and certified nurse assistant services, Thrive SPC focuses on supporting children with complex medical needs in the comfort of their homes.

name: Quay Australia
summary: Quay Australia is a socially-connected global eyewear brand with a passionate following. The company designs and markets affordable fashion sunglasses for trendsetters worldwide.

name: Sipartech
summary: Sipartech operates neutral dark fiber networks in the Paris metro area, Marseille and Lille. Through its own network, Sipartech provides high-capacity, low-latency connectivity to data centers, telecom operators and corporate clients.

name: Calypso Technology
summary: Calypso is a global provider of capital markets software. Its integrated platform enables financial trading, processing, risk management and accounting across multiple asset classes and is used by over 35,000 market professionals in 60+ countries.

name: InfoArmor
summary: InfoArmor provides identity and cyber intelligence services that help clients fight emerging fraud and advanced cyber threats. The company partners with employers, benefit brokers, consultants, and benefits exchanges to deliver identify theft solutions alongside existing employee benefits.

name: PharmScript
summary: PharmScript is a leading pharmacy provider to long-term care and post-acute care facilities, serving more than 500 facilities across 18 states. The company provides rigorous quality assurance, innovative technology and high-quality service to help healthcare facilities administer medications safely.

name: Vestmark
summary: Vestmark is the premier SaaS technology provider for comprehensive unified wealth solutions utilized by financial advisors and institutions. Its integrated platform helps advisors, broker dealers and asset managers to more efficiently deliver advice to their clients. Vestmark‘s client base is comprised of top tier financial institutions, and the platform currently services 1.5 million accounts with an aggregate of $500 billion in assets.

name: EHE Health
summary: EHE Health offers a managed healthcare service focused on prevention that drives validated clinical outcomes while delivering enterprise-wide value. EHE partners with employers to create a healthier, happier, and more engaged workforce through a nationwide network of physicians and digital tools.

name: Fuze
summary: Fuze (formerly ThinkingPhones) is a global, cloud-based unified communications platform that empowers productivity and delivers insights across the enterprise by enabling simplified business voice communications, flexible video conferencing, and always-on collaboration. Fuze enables a modern, mobile workforce to seamlessly communicate anytime, anywhere, across any device.

name: Perforce Software
summary: Founded in 1995, Perforce was a leading provider of version control and configuration management software for teams building digital products. After serving as CEO of Perforce for more than two decades, Founder Christopher Seiwald sought to transition the business to new leadership. Summit introduced software industry veteran Janet Dryer and in 2016, led a majority investment in Perforce to help launch the company on its next phase of growth.

name: Healthline Media
summary: Healthline Media provides health and wellness information to consumers through its media properties Healthline.com, MedicalNewsToday.com and Greatist.com. The company’s mission is to be its users’ most trusted ally in their pursuit of health and well-being by offering medically reviewed clinical content that is authoritative, approachable and actionable.

name: DMG Practice Management Solutions
summary: Founded in 1999, DMG Practice Management Solutions is a subsidiary of DuPage Medical Group (DMG), which had grown to become the largest independent, multi-specialty physician group in the Chicago area by 2015. DMG provided integrated physician-directed health care focused on quality, efficiency and access.

name: Siteimprove
summary: Siteimprove’s SaaS-based solutions provide automated content and analytics insights to help organizations transform the way they manage and deliver their digital presence. The Siteimprove Intelligence Platform offers complete visibility across online content quality, performance and visitor behavior, empowering more than 7,500 customers worldwide to achieve digital certainty.

name: Signavio
summary: Signavio is a provider of SaaS-based business process analysis and decision management software that helps businesses efficiently design, implement, analyse and manage complex processes, decisions and workflows.

name: Reverb.com
summary: Reverb is a rapidly growing online marketplace that connects buyers and sellers of new and used musical instruments and gear. On Reverb, users can easily create product listings, browse new and used gear, compare historical pricing and sales data, and also explore a wealth of content such as product reviews, demo videos, gear histories, and artist interviews.

name: Sézane
summary: Sézane is an online-only women’s wear fashion brand based in Paris. With a loyal customer following built entirely through word-of-mouth and social media, Sézane offers high-quality, ready-to-wear French chic style at an accessible price.

name: Mi9 Retail
summary: Mi9 Retail is a premier provider of integrated solutions for retail merchandising, e-commerce, point-of-sale, business intelligence and customer-centric store operations software. Acquired in 2014 by an experienced team of senior operators, Mi9 grew quickly, executing on management’s vision of becoming one of the largest, privately owned, software companies focused exclusively on retail.

name: Aeryon Labs
summary: Aeryon Labs is a leading provider of high-performance drones and software solutions for military, public safety, and commercial customers around the globe. Aeryon’s battle-tested Unmanned Aircraft Systems set the standard for real-time, secure, aerial intelligence across a wide range of mission-critical applications from clandestine tactical missions, to search and rescue operations, to commercial inspections and more.

name: PatSnap
summary: PatSnap provides a SaaS-based intellectual property (IP) analytics and management platform that allows both technical and business professionals to research, map, visualize and analyze the global IP landscape in any field of interest. PatSnap supports IP innovation across 40 different industries by providing its global customer base with timely, comprehensive intelligence that enables better analysis of the technology landscape.

name: TSheets
summary: Founded in 2006, TSheets provides cloud-based time tracking software to small- and medium-sized businesses. Thousands of companies in over 100 countries trust TSheets’ SaaS and mobile applications to track employee time and attendance for payroll and workforce management.

name: Darktrace
summary: Darktrace (LSE: DARK) is a global leader in cybersecurity artificial intelligence. Darktrace's technology continuously learns and updates its knowledge of a business’ data and digital environment and applies that understanding to autonomously spot and respond to known and unknown in-progress threats within seconds across the entire organization.

name: RELEX Solutions
summary: RELEX Solutions provides a unified supply chain and retail planning platform designed to align and optimize demand, merchandising, supply chain, operations, and production planning across the end-to-end value chain.

name: Advanced Cell Diagnostics
summary: Founded in 2006, Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) quickly established itself as a leader in the field of molecular pathology, developing cell- and tissue-based diagnostic tests for personalized medicine and life science research. With a rapidly growing product portfolio based on its proprietary RNAscope® technology, ACD sought an experienced life sciences investor to help expand the company’s go-to-market capability with a focus on international expansion and eCommerce enablement.

name: Paradigm
summary: Paradigm is an accountable specialty care management organization focused on improving the lives of people with complex and catastrophic injuries and diagnoses. The company has been a pioneer in value-based care since 1991 and has an exceptional track record of generating the very best outcomes for patients, payers, and providers. Deep clinical expertise is the foundation for every part of Paradigm's business, including its risk-based clinical solutions, case management services, high-value specialty networks, home health, and payment integrity programs.

name: InvoiceCloud
summary: Invoice Cloud provides SaaS-based solutions designed to simplify client engagement. The company serves multiple industries including healthcare, consumer finance, insurance, non-profit, local government and utilities, delivering digital engagement solutions that transform customer interactions into loyalty building opportunities by simplifying complex business processes and transactions.

name: DentalPro
summary: Founded in 2010, DentalPro owns and operates full-service dental clinics across Italy, most of which are conveniently located in shopping malls. By partnering with local dentists and providing comprehensive practice management support and training, DentalPro enables practitioners to focus on providing accessible and high-quality care to their patients.

name: Advance Health
summary: Advance Health provides in-home health risk assessments and chronic care management services and solutions through its extensive provider network and a proprietary mobile workflow technology.

name: Grand Design RV
summary: Don Clark, Bill Fenech and Ron Fenech founded Grand Design RV in 2012 to introduce innovation in the recreational vehicle market, offering high-quality, feature-packed towable fifth wheel RVs with an intense focus on customer and dealer satisfaction. Leveraging their combined 50+ years of experience in the RV industry, the founders built Grand Design from the ground up and were seeing tremendous growth with a unique business model that enabled industry-leading quality and the agility to quickly respond to fast-changing customer preferences.

name: Independent Vetcare
summary: Run by and for vets, IVC was formed in 2011 through the merger of several large independent veterinary hospitals across the United Kingdom. With a vet-focused business model that enables local practices to retain their independence while centralizing administrative functions and support services at the group level, IVC quickly grew to more than 100 veterinary hospitals.

name: MDVIP
summary: MDVIP was founded in 2000 with a vision for a better way to practice medicine and to provide level of care that patients deserve. Since its founding, the company has been at the forefront of affordable, personalized healthcare; MDVIP-affiliated physicians limit the size of their practices, allowing each physician to spend more time with patients than in a traditional practice model.

name: Uber
summary: Uber is an on-demand transportation service that is evolving the way the world moves. By seamlessly connecting riders to drivers through mobile and web apps, Uber makes cities more accessible, opening up more possibilities for riders and more business for drivers. With operations in hundreds of cities, Uber has a large and rapidly expanding global presence.

name: Masternaut
summary: Masternaut is a market-leading SaaS telematics platform that harnesses IoT to empower businesses and unlock greater operational efficiencies. More than 10,000 businesses have deployed Masternaut telematics in 250,000+ vehicles to keep their mobile workforce safe on the road, lower fuel consumption, increase productivity in their workforce, rationalize their fleet makeup, improve their customer experience, and reduce CO2 emissions.

name: BluePearl Veterinary Services
summary: BluePearl Veterinary Partners was formed in 2008 by brothers Darryl Shaw and Dr. Neil Shaw after merging their Tampa- and New York-based specialty veterinary hospitals with an existing specialty and emergency hospital in Kansas. Over the next several years, BluePearl continued to expand its operations, acquiring independent veterinarian-led specialty and emergency practices while maintaining a strong standard of care and great service to their referral base of primary care veterinarians.

name: ABILITY Network
summary: ABILITY Network is a national leader in providing innovative, web-based connectivity and workflow solutions that simplify clinical and administrative tasks for healthcare providers. Providers use the myABILITY platform daily to help them manage their administrative and clinical processes.

name: Wellcentive
summary: Founded in 2005, Wellcentive was built from the ground up to help physicians and health organizations transition to value-based care. The company's cloud-based, scalable population health management solutions were designed to simplify complex data from all points of care to advance comprehensive care management and payer collaboration, while improving clinical, financial and human outcomes.

name: NetBrain Technologies
summary: NetBrain is the market leader for network automation. Its software platform provides end-to-end network visibility across hybrid environments while automating tasks across IT workflows. NetBrain's software is used by enterprises and managed services providers to automate network management, accelerate troubleshooting and strengthen network security.

name: Integrated DNA Technologies
summary: Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT) is the world's largest manufacturer of custom nucleic acids (i.e., building blocks of DNA and RNA) and assists researchers in advancing genomics technology, including synthetic biology and Next-Generation Sequencing. The company serves more than 90,000 customers across a diverse set of end markets including academic research, biotechnology, clinical diagnostics and pharmaceuticals.

name: Parts Town
summary: With a commitment to customer service and innovation, and an industry-leading e-commerce platform, Parts Town had become one of the fastest-growing OEM parts distributors to the foodservice industry in North America.

name: Jamf
summary: Chip Pearson and Zach Halmstad founded Jamf in 2002 with a mission to help organizations of all sizes embrace Apple in the enterprise environment. As former Apple systems administrators, they understood the challenges and opportunities of the Apple environment, and focused on providing products centered around the user experience. Jamf developed an advanced set of enterprise-grade features and functionality, helping system administrators to confidently manage every Mac, iPhone or iPad in their environment via an elegant, flexible management platform.

name: Arista Networks
summary: Arista Networks delivers software driven cloud networking solutions for large datacenter and computing environments. The company's award-winning 10/40/100 GbE switches redefine scalability, robustness and price-performance, with 2000 customers and more than one million cloud networking ports being deployed worldwide.

name: Gainsight
summary: Gainsight helps businesses reduce churn, increase up-sell and drive customer success. The company's SaaS suite integrates with Salesforce and uses big data analytics to evaluate sources of customer intelligence including sales data, usage logs, support tickets and surveys.

name: Systems Maintenance Services
summary: Systems Maintenance Services (SMS) is a global provider of multivendor IT asset lifecycle services and support solutions for datacenters. SMS supports more than 3,000 clients globally across North America, Asia and Europe, including Cisco, IBM, HP, Dell, Oracle, EMC, Hitachi, NetApp, Lexmark and Xerox.

name: Clarabridge
summary: Clarabridge provides an omnichannel Customer Experience Management (CEM) SaaS platform that grew to serve hundreds of the world's most recognized companies and brands. With industry-leading technology, the company drove market expansion and enterprise adoption with a high growth, capital efficient business model. In 2021, Clarabridge was acquired by Qualtrics for $1.1 billion.

name: ModMed
summary: ModMed provides a powerful, cloud- and tablet-based electronic medical record (EMR) system to accommodate the specific needs of healthcare specialties. The company's product, Electronic Medical Assistant (EMA), offers a touch-based interface that adapts to providers' preferences, reduces documentation time, and contextualizes treatment patterns at the point-of-care.

name: A10 Networks
summary: A10 Networks provides high-performance networking and security solutions that help organizations accelerate, optimize and secure their web and enterprise-based software applications.

name: COMS Interactive
summary: COMS Interactive offers software solutions that help healthcare facilities more effectively treat and monitor their patient population. The company's software dramatically reduces hospital readmissions, improves patient quality of life, enhances communication among providers, and reduces payer costs.

name: Ruffalo Noel Levitz
summary: Ruffalo Noel Levitz provides technology-enabled enrollment and fundraising management services to a diverse array of non-profit postsecondary institutions.

name: Striim
summary: Striim uniquely combines both streaming data integration and streaming operational intelligence in a single platform with the mission to make data useful the instant it's born. The platform enables multi-stream data integration and real-time Change Data Capture (CDC) across a wide variety of data sources.

name: Visier
summary: Visier provides SaaS-based business intelligence applications focused on workforce analytics for HR departments. Visier's solutions are preconfigured to provide intuitive, highly visual, and interactive content out of the box.

name: Peak Well Systems
summary: Peak Well Systems (Peak) was established in 2002 to provide the upstream oil and gas industry with advanced well intervention technology to extend well life, restore well integrity and enhance performance. Ten years later and still independent, Peak had become a provider of choice for major oil companies, national oil companies and service companies around the world.

name: Philz Coffee
summary: Philz Coffee is a specialty coffee retailer with cafes in the San Francisco Bay Area. Philz offers its own specialized blends of coffee along with a customized and personal customer service experience.

name: Acacia Communications
summary: Acacia was founded in 2009 with a vision to lead the optical networking industry into the 100G+ era. As one of the first vendors to launch a 100G subsystem with coherent detection in 2011, Acacia established an early leadership position in a rapidly growing market, with demand driven by network upgrade cycles as communications providers work to meet exponentially rising bandwidth demands.

name: RiskIQ
summary: RiskIQ provides web-based security software that monitors websites and mobile app stores. The company's scanning engine crawls millions of web pages and mobile apps every day to identify threats such as malware, malvertising, phishing, click fraud and unauthorized brand usage.

name: Vivint Solar
summary: Vivint Solar is a leading provider of residential solar energy systems. The company designs, installs, and maintains cost-effective solar energy systems, enabling homeowners to enjoy all the benefits of solar energy with no up-front costs.

name: Vivint Smart Home
summary: Vivint Smart Home is a leading provider of smart home technology. The company delivers services through a cloud-based platform that integrates a wide range of wireless features and components to provide simple, affordable home security, energy management and home automation.

name: Zenoss
summary: Zenoss provides an IT observability and monitoring platform capable of handling physical, virtual and cloud-based IT environments.

name: Surgical Affiliates Management Group (SAMGI)
summary: SAMGI is a provider of general, orthopedic and trauma surgical hospitalist programs to acute-care hospitals. The company delivers comprehensive surgical care and administrative services to hospitals including 24/7 coverage, physician recruiting and performance management.

name: 360T Group
summary: Entrepreneur Carlo Kölzer founded 360T in 2000 and grew the business from a regional technology start-up into a leading global multi-bank trading platform designed to enable clients to trade with greater transparency and enhanced control. By 2012, Frankfurt-based 360T was highly profitable and growing, with more than 100 global and regional market makers connecting to its trading platform.

name: Westwing Group
summary: Westwing is the leading brand and platform in home & living eCommerce. Through its 'shoppable magazine', Westwing inspires its customers with a curated product selection (textiles, furniture, rugs, lamps, kitchenware, etc.) and combines that with gorgeous content. Westwing's mission is: To inspire and to make every home a beautiful home. The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Munich.

name: Rocket Fuel
summary: Rocket Fuel is a leading provider of artificial intelligence advertising solutions that transform digital media campaigns into self-optimizing engines that learn and adapt in real-time and deliver outstanding results from awareness to sales.

name: Progressive Finance
summary: Progressive Finance is a provider of web-based lease-to-own financing programs for retailers.

name: Lazada
summary: Lazada is Southeast Asia's fastest growing online department store, with operations in Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Thailand and Vietnam. The company has a broad product offering in categories ranging from consumer electronics to household goods, toys and sports equipment.

name: Global Fashion Group
summary: Global Fashion Group (GFG) operates five leading fashion e-commerce brands across five emerging market regions: Dafiti (Brazil), Lamoda (Russia), Zalora (Southeast Asia), Namshi (Middle East) and The Iconic (Australia). Offering a wide assortment of leading international and local fashion brands, as well as a selection of internal brands, GFG currently operates in over 20 countries.

name: Infor
summary: Formed in 2002, Infor is one of the world's largest providers of enterprise application software to large and mid-sized companies across numerous industry sectors. The company's highly targeted vertical software automates and integrates critical business processes, helping customers to better manage their suppliers, partners, customers and employees. Summit originally invested in Infor in 2003 and 2004 when Infor acquired two of Summit's prior portfolio companies. Several years later, with a new and talented management team in place led by CEO Charles Phillips, Infor was poised to extend its leadership position in vertical-specific business applications while transitioning its offerings to the cloud. Based on our long-standing relationship with the company, Summit had the opportunity to complete a new and larger equity investment, and Infor chose to partner with Summit again in 2012.

name: Delphix
summary: Delphix provides database virtualization solutions for agile data management. Leading global organizations use Delphix to dramatically reduce the time, cost, and risk of application rollouts, simplifying database operations while consolidating redundant hardware.

name: Telesign
summary: TeleSign is the leading provider of phone-based authentication and mobile identity solutions for global businesses that seek to prevent account and transaction fraud and protect their networks and customers from malicious behavior.

name: Solutionreach
summary: Solutionreach is a leading patient engagement and communication platform for medical, dental and vision practices. The company's solutions empower healthcare practices to save time and generate more revenue while transforming the patient experience.

name: Help Systems
summary: Founded in 1982, HelpSystems is a market-leading provider of IT management software solutions that enable IT professionals to automate and simplify server and network management, protect data, and give people simple access to information they need. Summit initially partnered with CEO Janet Dryer and invested in HelpSystems in 2005, and the business was acquired by Audax Group in 2007. Poised for new growth opportunities in 2012, Dryer and her management team chose to partner again with Summit to support continued expansion of HelpSystems products and services on the global stage.

name: Wowza Media Systems
summary: Wowza Media Systems develops high-performance media server software that enables service providers, media properties, enterprises, and other organizations to deliver video and other rich media content to any digital device, including computer, mobile or TV.

name: Logi Analytics
summary: Logi Analytics is a provider of web-based business intelligence software. The company's solutions deliver dashboards, user-friendly reports, and on-demand analysis that enable users to make better decisions for their organizations.

name: Continuum
summary: Continuum provides a leading SaaS-based platform that Managed Services Providers use to efficiently monitor, troubleshoot and maintain desktops, servers and other endpoints for small- and medium-sized businesses.

name: Carecentrix
summary: CareCentrix provides specialty benefit management services to health plans, improving quality and reducing costs in several areas of home-based healthcare such as home nursing, home medical equipment, home drug infusion, sleep diagnostic tests and hospital readmissions.

name: Access Information Management
summary: Access Information Management is a records and information management (RIM) services provider to businesses across the United States. The company offers a complete suite of RIM services including records management, data protection (electronic computer media), secure destruction, and Digital Access Solutions.

name: Solid State Equipment
summary: Solid State Equipment manufactures single wafer wet processing and cleaning equipment for the semiconductor industry.

name: Bigpoint
summary: Bigpoint is a European online games platform that develops and publishes browser-based games distributed to more than 200 million registered users in over 30 languages.

name: Hiperos
summary: Hiperos is a leading provider of a SaaS platform used by large enterprises to manage all of their third party relationships.

name: M A Com Technology Solutions
summary: MACOM designs and manufactures high-performance semiconductor products for the telecommunications, industrial and defense, and datacenter industries. The company services over 6,000 customers annually with a broad product portfolio that incorporates RF, microwave, analog and mixed signal and optical semiconductor technologies.

name: Clearwater Analytics
summary: Clearwater Analytics provides web-based software for investment portfolio reporting, accounting and analytics for corporate treasuries, asset managers, insurance companies, custody banks and electronic trading portals.

name: My Dentist
summary: My Dentist Holdings is a retail dental practice management company that owns and operates more than 20 free-standing office locations in the United States. The company provides general dentistry, cosmetic dentistry, dentures, implants and orthodontics.

name: Winshuttle
summary: Winshuttle provides ERP usability software, enabling business users to work with ERP systems directly from Excel, PDF, SharePoint and other interfaces without any programming.

name: Avast
summary: Avast Software began as a software cooperative in former Czechoslovakia. By 2010 – more than 25 years later – the company had built a leading antivirus software, protecting millions of users from online threats. With its disruptive freemium business model, Avast was growing strongly and profitably, and sought a global investor to provide strategic advice and provide partial liquidity for shareholders. In 2010, Avast chose to partner with Summit.

name: Accedian Networks
summary: Accedian Networks was founded in 2005 with a focus on delivering real-time end-to-end network visibility and optimization solutions to help carriers and service providers alleviate performance issues resulting from ever-increasing traffic on wireless and enterprise networks. As carriers competed to attract and retain their customers, many – including T-Mobile, Verizon Wireless and AT&T – turned to Accedian's performance assurance solutions to dramatically improve the end-user experience. Accedian's management team sought an experienced equity partner to help manage this rapid growth, strengthen the balance sheet and penetrate new Tier-1 customers.

name: Acturis
summary: Theo Duchen and David McDonald founded Acturis with the goal of driving inefficiencies out of the insurance sector by focusing on technology and process innovation. Leveraging their backgrounds in the insurance and ecommerce practices at McKinsey, the founders successfully bootstrapped Acturis to become the leading SaaS-based insurance technology provider in Europe. The founders sought an experienced partner to help internationalize the business.

name: Ogone
summary: Ogone became a leader in payment processing in Europe by focusing on solving the complex problem of enabling online merchants to offer multiple forms of preferred payment methods across borders. The company sought to accelerate its growth through international expansion and acquisitions, and management chose to partner with Summit in 2010.

name: Casa Systems
summary: Casa Systems offers a new category of network edge devices for interactive digital video and broadband IP services. The company provides next generation cable modem transmission systems and intelligent video processing solutions for broadcast and unicast services worldwide.

name: Wildfire Interactive
summary: Founded by Victoria Ransom and Alain Chuard, Wildfire was a pioneer in the category of social media marketing software, and built a large customer base that included Facebook, AT&T, Pepsi, Sony, Unilever and Yahoo. Profitable nearly from the start, the bootstrapped company was growing 20% per month when the founders sought an experienced software investor who could help them scale rapidly while preserving Wildfire's culture and capital-efficient operating strategy.

name: Ob Hospitalist Group
summary: Ob Hospitalist Group develops and manages in-house Ob/Gyn hospitalist programs. The company delivers comprehensive Ob/Gyn clinical solutions and administrative services including 24/7 coverage, physician recruiting, billing support and performance management.

name: Ubiquiti
